heroDescription.processed: Hearthwood creates heirloom-quality furniture from sustainably sourced hardwoods. Every piece is handcrafted in our Vermont workshop by skilled artisans who take pride in joinery, grain selection, and finishes that reveal the natural beauty of wood.

body.processed:
Clean lines meet warm wood in our Vermont Modern collection. Inspired by mid-century design principles and crafted with traditional joinery, these pieces bridge the gap between contemporary aesthetics and timeless craftsmanship.

Each piece features exposed mortise-and-tenon joints, hand-rubbed oil finishes, and carefully bookmatched wood grain that showcases the natural character of American hardwoods.

Available Pieces

  • Dining tables (seats 4-12)
  • Sideboards and buffets
  • Platform beds and nightstands
  • Desks and media consoles
  • Coffee and end tables
body.summary: Clean lines meet warm wood in our Vermont Modern collection. Inspired by mid-century design principles and crafted with traditional joinery, these pieces bridge the gap between contemporary aesthetics

image.alt: Modern solid wood dining table in a bright room

entity.title: Farmstead

body.processed:
Rooted in New England's agricultural heritage, the Farmstead collection celebrates the honest beauty of reclaimed and naturally distressed wood. These pieces bring warmth and character to any space with their generous proportions and rustic elegance.

Many Farmstead pieces incorporate reclaimed barn wood from historic Vermont structures, giving each piece a unique story and patina that cannot be replicated.

Available Pieces

  • Harvest tables and farm benches
  • Hutches and pie safes
  • Barn door media cabinets
  • Trestle desks
  • Storage benches and coat racks
body.summary: Rooted in New England's agricultural heritage, the Farmstead collection celebrates the honest beauty of reclaimed and naturally distressed wood. These pieces bring warmth and character to any space wi

entity.tagline: Honest furniture with history and heart

image.alt: Rustic farmhouse dining table with bench seating

body.processed:
Designed for modern living spaces where every square foot counts, the Studio collection delivers the same Hearthwood quality in space-conscious proportions. Multifunctional designs, integrated storage, and modular options make these pieces perfect for apartments and smaller homes.

Every Studio piece can be customized to your exact dimensions, ensuring a perfect fit for your space.

Available Pieces

  • Compact dining sets
  • Wall-mounted desks and shelving
  • Storage beds with drawers
  • Nesting tables
  • Modular shelving systems

image.alt: Minimalist desk and shelving in a modern apartment

body.processed:
This living room design pairs our Vermont Modern coffee table and media console with a neutral sofa and warm textiles to create a space that's both stylish and inviting. The black walnut pieces anchor the room while allowing the natural light to shine.

The key to this look is balance: clean-lined furniture paired with soft textures like linen, wool, and natural fiber rugs. Plants and ceramics add organic warmth without clutter.

body.processed:
Gather around our Farmstead harvest table for meals that bring people together. This dining room design seats ten comfortably and showcases the beauty of reclaimed wood against whitewashed walls and industrial lighting.

The matching farm bench on one side and Windsor chairs on the other create an informal, welcoming atmosphere perfect for family dinners and holiday gatherings.

entity.title: Studio Apartment Bedroom

body.processed:
Maximize your small space without sacrificing style. Our Studio collection storage bed with built-in drawers eliminates the need for a separate dresser, while the wall-mounted nightstand shelves keep the floor clear for a spacious feel.

The white oak pieces brighten the room and pair beautifully with soft linens and minimalist decor. Every inch of this design is intentional and functional.

entity.title: Anna Lindberg

body.processed:
Anna is the creative force behind Hearthwood's most iconic collections, including Vermont Modern. Trained in Scandinavian furniture design, she brings a deep understanding of form, function, and materiality to every piece she creates.

Her philosophy centers on the idea that great furniture should disappear into daily life -- beautiful enough to admire, comfortable enough to forget. Anna's designs have been featured in Dwell, Architectural Digest, and the Smithsonian Cooper Hewitt Design Museum.
body.summary: Anna is the creative force behind Hearthwood's most iconic collections, including Vermont Modern. Trained in Scandinavian furniture design, she brings a deep understanding of form, function, and mater

entity.education: Royal Danish Academy of Fine Arts

body.processed:
Robert is our master craftsman and workshop director, responsible for translating design concepts into production-ready pieces. A fourth-generation woodworker, he learned traditional joinery from his grandfather and has spent three decades perfecting techniques that make Hearthwood furniture structurally superior.

Under Robert's direction, our workshop produces furniture using time-honored methods -- mortise-and-tenon joints, dovetails, and hand-rubbed finishes -- that ensure every piece will last for generations.

entity.title: Mei Tanaka

body.processed:
Mei leads our interior design consultation service, helping clients select and arrange Hearthwood pieces to transform their living spaces. With a background in architecture and interior design, she has an exceptional eye for proportion, flow, and the interplay between furniture and space.

Mei's room designs have been published in House Beautiful and she regularly speaks at design conferences about sustainable furniture in modern interiors.

entity.education: Parsons School of Design, New York

body.processed:
Hearthwood Furniture was founded in 1984 by Erik and Ingrid Lindberg in a converted barn in Shelburne, Vermont. What began as a two-person operation making custom tables for local restaurants has grown into one of America's most respected solid wood furniture makers, with a 60-person workshop and a 15,000 square foot showroom in Burlington.

Our Craft

Every Hearthwood piece is made by hand in our Vermont workshop. We use traditional joinery -- mortise-and-tenon, dovetail, and tongue-and-groove -- because these methods create furniture that's stronger and more durable than anything made with screws, nails, or glue alone. Our finishes are hand-rubbed oils and lacquers that protect the wood while allowing its natural beauty to shine.

Sustainability

We source 100% of our wood from sustainably managed forests and FSC-certified suppliers. Our workshop is solar-powered, and we repurpose every scrap of wood as heating fuel or donate it to local schools and makers. Furniture that lasts generations is the most sustainable choice of all.

body.processed:
How long will my order take?

Standard collection pieces ship within 4-6 weeks. Custom orders require 6-8 weeks. During peak season (September-December), lead times may extend by 1-2 weeks.

Do you offer delivery?

Yes! We offer white-glove delivery throughout the Northeast. We carefully deliver your furniture to the room of your choice, assemble any components, and remove all packaging. Nationwide delivery is available through our freight partners.

What kind of wood do you use?

We work primarily with American hardwoods -- black walnut, white oak, cherry, maple, and ash. All of our wood comes from sustainably managed forests and FSC-certified suppliers.

Can I customize a piece?

Absolutely. We offer custom sizing, wood species selection, and finish options on all of our collections. Contact our design team to discuss your specific needs and receive a custom quote.
